skill.name: req-change-workflow
skill.description: Standardize requirement/feature changes in an existing codebase (especially Chrome extensions) by turning "改需求/需求变更/调整交互/改功能/重构流程" into a repeatable loop: clarify acceptance criteria, confirm current behavior from code, assess impact/risk, design the new logic, implement with small diffs, run a fixed regression checklist, and update docs/decision log. Use when the user feels the change process is chaotic, when edits tend to sprawl across files, or when changes touch manifest/service worker/OAuth/storage/UI and need reliable verification + rollback planning.
skill.summary: A structured 7-step workflow for managing requirement changes in existing codebases with gated approvals and regression checklists.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: All 42 static findings are false positives. The "Ruby/shell backtick execution" flags are misidentified markdown code formatting. The "weak cryptographic algorithm" and "system reconnaissance" flags are text misinterpretations. The skill is a legitimate requirement change workflow for Chrome extensions with no malicious intent.

content.user_title: Structure requirement changes with gated workflow
content.value_statement: Managing requirement changes in existing codebases often leads to scope creep and untested side effects. This skill provides a gated 7-step workflow that ensures user approval at each stage before implementation starts.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through a 7-step gated workflow: clarify, baseline, impact, design, implement, validate, document
Generates structured change briefs with acceptance criteria and rollback plans
Provides regression checklists for Chrome extension testing
Produces decision logs for future maintainability
Scans codebase for relevant files using keyword search
limitations: Requires manual verification steps for Chrome extension reload
Does not automate test execution; focuses on workflow guidance
Designed primarily for Chrome extensions; may need adaptation for other project types

use_cases.title: Feature scope clarification
use_cases.description: When a stakeholder requests a new feature or behavior change, use this workflow to lock scope and get explicit approval before writing any code.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers working with product managers or designers

use_cases.title: Regression prevention for extensions
use_cases.description: Before implementing changes to manifest.json or service_worker.js, run the regression checklist to ensure existing functionality remains intact.

use_cases.title: Decision traceability
use_cases.description: Document why a particular design was chosen over alternatives, creating a maintainable decision log for future team members.

prompt_templates.title: Start a requirement change
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to change [describe the feature/behavior]. Help me use the req-change-workflow to structure this change.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - starting a new requirement change

prompt_templates.title: Clarify existing change scope
prompt_templates.prompt: The requirement has shifted to [describe new requirements]. Help me re-clarify the scope using the change brief template.

prompt_templates.title: Review proposed design
prompt_templates.prompt: Here is my current understanding: [describe current behavior]. I want to change it to: [describe desired behavior]. Show me the proposed design before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Run regression checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: I have completed the implementation. Run the regression checklist and document the test results.

output_examples.input: I want to add a dark mode toggle to the extension
output: Change Brief generated with target, out-of-scope items, acceptance criteria, and rollback expectations
Current behavior baseline documented with file list
Impact & risk assessment with manual validation checkpoints

output_examples.input: The OAuth flow needs to refresh tokens automatically
output: 7-step implementation plan created with gated approval points
Design proposal with state model and failure recovery
Decision log entry documenting why token refresh was chosen over silent re-auth

best_practices: Always get user approval at Step 4 before writing any code
Use the regression checklist after any manifest.json or service_worker.js change
Document the rollback plan before implementation to ensure reversibility
anti_patterns: Skipping directly to implementation without clarifying acceptance criteria
Making changes across multiple entrypoints without centralizing logic first
Ignoring the regression checklist and assuming small changes have no side effects

faq.question: When should I use this workflow?
faq.answer: Use this workflow when requirement changes feel chaotic, edits spread across many files, or changes affect manifest, service worker, OAuth, storage, or UI.

faq.question: Do I need to follow all 7 steps?
faq.answer: The workflow recommends all 7 steps, but Step 0 (plan creation) is optional. Steps 1-4 require user approval before proceeding.

faq.question: Can this workflow be used for non-Chrome extension projects?
faq.answer: Yes, the core workflow applies to any codebase. The regression checklist references are specific to Chrome extensions but can be replaced with appropriate checklists.

faq.question: What if the user rejects the proposed design?
faq.answer: Return to Step 3 or 4 with the feedback and revise the design proposal until the user approves before implementation.

faq.question: How do I handle changes that affect multiple modules?
faq.answer: Document all affected files in Step 3. Centralize logic in one module when possible and keep ES module imports explicit.

faq.question: What if the rollback is not a simple revert?
faq.answer: If the change requires data migration or backfill, explicitly document the migration strategy and include it in the acceptance criteria before proceeding.
